name: Trap-3 Raise (Prone Y at 135°)
description: Lie face-down on an incline bench (~30-45°) holding light dumbbells. Arms extended toward the floor with thumbs pointing UP (external rotation). Lift the arms in a Y shape — at roughly 135° from the torso, NOT straight overhead. Raise until arms are in line with ears or slightly above, hold 1-2s at the top, slow eccentric back to neutral. The 135° angle isolates the lower trap (the underused 'trap 3' division — vs. trap 1 = upper trap, trap 2 = mid trap). Common cue: 'thumbs up, arms in a Y, lift from the SHOULDER BLADES not the hands.' Start light — 5-10lb is plenty. Lower trap is small and fatigues fast. Often prescribed for shoulder-impingement rehab, scapular dyskinesia, and chronic upper-trap dominance.
